summary.intent: Address Codex review comments on PR #665 in fogodev/ars-ui by fixing selection mutation guards in the Listbox and Select components, then push and re-request review until CI and Codex pass cleanly.
summary.outcome: Committed two fix batches — `21225b95` resolving six stale/missing-key, disabled-state, and hidden-input-name threads, and a follow-up commit 'fix(components): guard structural selection keys' covering structural collection keys, typeahead defaults, select-all edge cases, and range anchors. All six original threads were replied to and resolved; local fmt, unit tests, proptest (256 cases), i18n, and clippy passed before the second push.

repo: Listbox and Select share selection mutation patterns but diverge at serialization: Select has a hidden input whose `name` attribute must be omitted when `Props::name` is `None`.
`Set::All` can be incorrectly materialized if stale or structurally non-focusable keys are processed before the guard; both `DeselectItem` paths need an early key-existence check.
The collection can contain structural (non-focusable) node types (groups, sections) whose keys must not participate in selection mutations or typeahead matching.
PropTest suite lives in `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines` and requires `--ignored` to run; 256 cases is the standard CI-equivalent count.
The repo uses nightly Rust for formatting (`cargo +nightly fmt --all`) but stable for builds and clippy.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment via `gh pr comment <n> --body '@codex review'`.
Review threads are resolved via the GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation using the thread node ID (prefix `PRRT_`).

code.finding: SelectItem and ToggleItem must guard that the key exists in the current collection before mutating selection state; stale events otherwise corrupt the selection set.
ItemActivated must check the whole-listbox disabled state in addition to per-item disabled; without this, activating a disabled listbox item bypasses the guard.
Range-selection anchor recomputation must skip structural/non-focusable collection nodes; previous logic could anchor on a group header key.
Select SelectItem short-circuits on missing keys before selection mutation and before hidden-input serialization to prevent stale state from updating the form value.
Hidden input `name` attribute must be conditionally omitted when `Props::name` is `None`; previously it was always emitted, producing a nameless form field.
Default typeahead keydown handler in Select must exclude structural collection nodes from candidate matching to avoid navigating to non-interactive items.

workflow: Reply-and-resolve review threads in a single `gh api` batch script to avoid rate-limit round-trips; resolving via GraphQL node ID is separate from the REST reply.
CI polling with progressive sleep intervals (120 → 240 → 300 → 360 s) avoids hammering the API while staying responsive to failures.
Run the full local gate (`fmt --check`, unit tests, proptest, i18n feature check, clippy -D warnings) before each push rather than relying on CI to catch regressions.

When a second review batch arrives during a CI wait, it is more efficient to address the new findings immediately and push a combined fix rather than waiting for CI to finish on a commit that will need another push anyway.

friction: CI jobs (unit tests, mutual exclusion) took 6+ minutes per run, requiring multiple multi-hundred-second polling loops and significant idle wall time.
Codex delivered review findings in two separate batches rather than one, forcing a second push cycle while the first CI run was still in progress.
Identifying which listbox/select code paths needed structural-key guards required multiple exploratory `sed`/`rg` reads across large files before the patch locations were clear.
open_items: Second push ('fix(components): guard structural selection keys') had re-triggered Codex review (`@codex review`) but the transcript ends before that review completes — a third batch of findings may be pending.
Unit-test and mutual-exclusion CI jobs were still running when the session ended; their results against the second commit are unknown.
Typeahead handler changes were patched but no explicit test name for the structural-node typeahead exclusion was mentioned — coverage may be incomplete.
